title: Lesson 7 — Praise and Blame

words.definition: to praise highly, especially in public
words.example: Critics lauded the film as an instant masterpiece.
She extolled the virtues of rising before dawn.

words.definition: a speech or piece of writing that praises someone highly, typically one who has died
words.example: His eulogy for his mentor moved the whole room to tears.

words.definition: an award or expression of praise
words.example: The novel earned every accolade it received.
The plan finally met with the committee's approbation.
The senator faced censure for the leaked remarks.
The coach castigated the team for its sloppy play.

words.definition: to criticize unfairly; to belittle or blacken the reputation of
words.example: He denigrated rivals instead of defending his own record.

words.definition: to regard or represent as being of little worth; to belittle
words.example: She never disparaged a beginner's honest question.

words.definition: to speak or write about in an abusively disparaging way
words.example: The tabloids vilified him before any facts came out.

words.definition: a sharp expression of disapproval or criticism
words.example: A stern rebuke from the judge silenced the lawyer.

words.definition: an expression of disapproval or disappointment
words.example: Her only reproach was a single disappointed look.

story: At the ceremony the host [[laud|lauded]] the young director and [[extol|extolled]] her debut, in a speech that sounded almost like a [[eulogy|eulogy]] for the old way of making films. The [[accolade|accolades]] poured in, and even the stingiest critics offered rare [[approbation|approbation]]. A year later the mood flipped. A leaked memo brought public [[censure|censure]]; reviewers who had cheered began to [[castigate|castigate]] her, and gossip columns rushed to [[denigrate|denigrate]] and [[disparage|disparage]] the very work they once praised. A few tried to [[vilify|vilify]] her outright. Her old mentor offered only a quiet [[rebuke|rebuke]], a gentle [[reproach|reproach]] that stung more than every headline combined.
